description: §31 E-DG-002 NEGATIVE — a reactive cell with a clean, unambiguous reader SHALL NOT draw E-DG-002. `<greeting> = "hello"` is declared at file scope (V5-strict structural form) and read by a plain markup interpolation `${@greeting}` — the single most direct consumption there is: a `reads` edge from the render node to the reactive node, with dependency wiring trivially established. E-DG-002 stays silent, and so does the whole E-DG- family. This NEG is the load-bearing baseline for E-DG-002's known false-positive CLASS — the corpus records repeated regressions where a genuinely-consumed cell false-fired "declared but never consumed" (SPEC.md:1009 `<span : @label>`; known-gaps `g-dg-class-attr-interp-not-consumed` for a class-attr interpolation read; Bug 74 for `/>` + `:`-shorthand; C9 for a read inside a derived `.filter()` arrow). Pinning the plain-interpolation reader guards the one form all of those degrade from. NOTE: the paired POSITIVE case (dg-002-no-readers-pos) is deliberately NOT authored — E-DG-002's fire condition + severity are a standing SPEC divergence (§34 catalog row SPEC.md:18285 says Severity Error and a READ-based trigger; impl#1 emits severity "warning" on a NEVER-READ cell at dependency-graph.ts:3182-3202; and §22.6 SPEC.md:15863-15866 contradicts the §34 row). This case's ABSENCE assertion is sound under BOTH readings and takes no side on that divergence. Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 ignored by superset.
rationale: §31.2: 'The graph SHALL represent all reactive dependencies: every expression that reads a `@variable` is a dependent edge on that variable.' A markup interpolation `${@greeting}` IS such an expression, so the graph SHALL carry the edge and the cell is a consumed cell. §22.6 (SPEC.md:15863-15866) fixes the sense in which E-DG-002 is keyed on consumption — 'An `@variable` that is declared elsewhere in the program and read inside a `^{}` runtime meta block SHALL be considered consumed for the purposes of E-DG-002 detection. The compiler SHALL NOT emit E-DG-002 for an `@variable` that is read exclusively inside one or more `^{}` runtime meta blocks.' If a read confined to a meta block already SHALL suppress the diagnostic, then a fortiori a read in an ordinary render context SHALL suppress it. The absence holds under the §34 catalog row's alternative reading too (SPEC.md:18285 — a read 'in a context whose dependency wiring cannot be safely established, e.g. dynamic computed-key dependency'): a static, non-computed `${@greeting}` interpolation wires fine-grained, so that trigger is not met either. Under every reading of the code the compiler SHALL stay silent here.
